excerpts.text:
MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual   
15

8. COMMISSIONING 
1. Check all wiring and connections. Do not place the fuses yet! 
2. Set the safety relay to REMOTE OFF, see page 12. 
3. Open MasterAdjust/CZone Configuration Tool, to configure the required events/circuits.

WARNING! 
If multiple charging devices are used to charge the battery (for instance both an 
AC powered charger and an alternator) then the Stop charge circuits/events must 
be configured for each charging device. In case of a multiple unit configuration, 
this must be done for each battery. 
If an alternator is part of the charging system, configure the Battery safety event. 
MLI Ultra event examples:

The settings depend on the used charger because different chargers have different state 
options. Check the manual of the used charger for how to switch the charging state. 
Depending on the installation in which the battery is used, programming of additional 
circuits/events may be required. 
For more information on MasterBus events see page 23. 
For more information on CZone circuits see page 28 and following. 
4. Test the safety relay (MasterAdjust only). 
Go to the monitoring page o

4) Trigger point for battery safety event. 
Resettable battery safety event; occurs when one of the following conditions is true: 
12V battery 
Any temperature: >15V 10s

<5°C / 41°F: <10.75V 10s 
>10°C / 50°F: <12V 10s 
24V battery 
Any temperature: >30V 10s 
<5°C / 41°F: <21.5V 10s 
>10°C / 50°F: <24V 10s 
Individual cell 
Any temperature: >3.8V 1s 
<5°C / 41°F: <2.7V 10s or <2.4V 1s 
>10°C / 50°F: <3.0V 10s or <2.7V 1s 
>65°C / 149°F: Any voltage (immediate)

Non-resettable battery safety event; occurs when one of the following conditions is true: 
12V battery 
Any temperature: >15.5V 1s

<5°C / 41°F: <10V 10s 
>10°C / 50°F: <11V 10s 
24V battery 
Any temperature: >31V 1s 
<5°C / 41°F: <20V 10s 
>10°C / 50°F: <22V 10s 
Individual cell 
Any temperature: >3.85V 1s 
<5°C / 41°F: <2.4V 10s or <2.2V 1s

4 Mounting strap location 
5 MasterBus / CZone connection (see sections Network wiring on pages 18 and 27) 
When waterproof protection is required, Mastervolt can also supply waterproof cable 
glands.

CAUTION! 
Make sure that the protective caps are correctly attached if the Auxiliary 
and/or MasterBus/CZone connectors are not used! 
6 Battery Safety connector, see the Installation drawing on page 11 (single), 35 (parallel) or

(5) FeedBack signal relay

minus)

2 short blinks, long pause; CZone identification in progress; 
Steady blinking; firmware update in p

excerpts.query: troubleshooting alarms faults error codes emergency protection
excerpts.text:
On the MasterBus control panel, check that the voltage is within limits; higher than 10V (20V) 
and lower than 15.5V (31V) and within temperature range; 0°C - 50°C / 32°F – 122°F. 
2. If within limits, click the "Close relay" button on the MasterBus control panel. Start charging if 
voltage is low (10V/20V) or discharging when high (15.5V/31V). 
3. If the relay trips again, a non-resettable battery safety event occurred. Do not try to force or 
reset the safety relay again! Isolate the battery and contact a Mastervolt representative.

excerpts.text:
18   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

13. MASTERBUS 
About MasterBus 
All devices that are compatible with MasterBus are marked with the MasterBus symbol. 
MasterBus is a CAN based, fully decentralized data network for communication between Mastervolt 
devices. MasterBus is used as power management system for all connected equipment, such as the 
inverter, battery charger, generator and many more. 
Every device that is compatible with MasterBus is equipped with two data ports. The devices are 
simply daisy chained together, forming a local data network. Monitoring panels such as the 
SmartRemote or EasyView 5 can be used for monitoring and control of all connected MasterBus 
equipment.

CAUTION! 
Never connect a non-MasterBus device to the MasterBus network directly! This will 
void warranty of all MasterBus devices connected. 
How to set up a MasterBus network 
- Connections between the devices are made by standard straight MasterBus cables. Mastervolt 
can supply these cables. 
- Up to 63 MasterBus devices can be interconnected. 
- MasterBus needs a terminating device on both ends of the network. 
- The electric power for the network comes from

Storage in months*  
* Provided that MasterBus power is off and there is no communication to the BMS.

DIP switch settings 
DIP SWITCH MEANING 
1 1 = MasterBus 
0 = CZone 
2 1 = MasterBus power ON 
0 = MasterBus power OFF or CZone 
3 to 10 CZone's unique address 
(DIP switches 3 to 10 are not used in MasterBus)

- Keep at least 1cm [3/8"] between two Li-Ion batteries for air flow. 
- The MLI Ultra must be secured to its foundation with the two included straps. 
- If the MLI Ultra is installed in a network, take the network powering into consideration. 
Required tools 
- Hexagon socket wrench (13mm / ½") 
- Flat blade screwdriver to fasten the relay wiring (2mm / 1/16") 
- MasterBus USB interface 
- Windows laptop or notebook with free MasterAdjust software or free CZone configuration tool.  
MasterAdjust software can be downloaded at www.mastervolt.com 
The CZone Configuration Tool software is available to technical CZone customers from the CZone 
Portal at downloads.czone.net

If the battery will not be used for a period exceeding 3 months, we advise the following: 
• If external AC power is available switch off all loads and switch on the charger. Apply a float 
voltage as specified in the following table. 
Model  Float voltage setting 
12V  13.5V 
24V  27.0V 
• If no external AC power is available: 
- Charge the battery to > 80% of its capacity before storage. 
- Set the safety relay knob to “LOCK OFF”, see page 12.  
- Make sure MasterBus powering is not set to "Always on" (see Configuration tab in 
MasterAdjust).  
In this setup the batteries can be kept at least 6 months without maintenance. However, it is highly 
recommended to charge the battery to > 80% of its capacity every 100 days.

excerpts.text:
MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual   
17

9. MAINTENANCE 
No specific maintenance is required. If necessary, use a soft, clean cloth to clean the MLI Ultra. 
Never use any liquids or acids. 
For reliable and optimum function examine your electrical installation on a regular base, at least 
once a year. Defects such as loose connections, damaged wiring etc. must be corrected 
immediately. 
10. DECOMMISSIONING 
If it is necessary to take the MLI Ultra out of operation, follow the instructions in order of succession 
as described below: 
1. Turn AC power off. 
2. Disconnect all loads and charging devices. 
3. Move the safety relay(s) in the LOCK OFF position, see page 12. 
4. Disconnect all wiring. 
11. REPLACEMENTS 
If MLI Ultra batteries need to be replaced individually, make sure that the open circuit voltage of 
these batteries equals the voltage of the replaced or paralleled batteries. 
In case of a MasterBus network: when exchanging charging devices that are used to charge the 
battery, the event configuration (such as the Stop Charge event), as well as other settings (such as 
charge voltage settings) needs to be redone for every new device. 
12.

assembled_user_prompt:
You extract an interaction profile for one piece of marine equipment from excerpts
of its official manual(s). You record observable facts about how humans interact
with the device. You do NOT judge importance or guide tier. You do NOT write guest
prose.

Rules:

1. Use ONLY the manual text provided. No general knowledge about this product or
   brand. Unmentioned = empty array / false / "unclear". Never guess.
   device.category_freeform must be the manual's own product category words
   (e.g. "charger/inverter", "MPPT solar charger") — never registry/taxonomy
   tokens such as electrical_dc, propulsion, freshwater_system.

2. Empty is meaningful. A busbar has no control surfaces and no operator actions;
   emit empty arrays.

3. audience per action, by the manual's own framing:
   - routine steps addressed to "you" → operator
   - "consult your dealer", "qualified personnel", installation-chapter procedures,
     DIP-switch / DIP table configuration → installer_or_technician
     (DIP configuration is always installer_or_technician + commissioning)
   - genuinely both → either

4. context per action: daily / situational / emergency / commissioning /
   maintenance. One procedure fitting two contexts → two entries.

5. Split actions: each action string is one verb phrase with one context.
   "monitor and configure via the app" → two actions (e.g. monitor → daily;
   configure → situational or commissioning per the manual's framing).
   Splitting bundled actions must never remove actions. Every distinct
   procedure the manual addresses to the operator must appear, including
   shutdown, restart, and fault/error-code procedures.
   has_emergency_procedure: true when the manual has an error-code / fault
   table or protective-disconnect recovery — not merely because an orderly
   service/storage shutdown exists (see calibration J).

6. control_surfaces on the base product vs. accessories sold separately: mark the
   latter optional_accessory: true. If a capability requires another product, also
   add a requires_devices entry with needed_for pointing at that field path
   (e.g. "control_surfaces[0]" or "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network").
   Never set needed_for to "" / null. Never attach requires_devices to a surface
   with optional_accessory: false (built-in capabilities do not "require" an
   accessory). Multiple requires_devices entries that share the same needed_for
   are OR alternatives (any one present on the vessel activates the capability);
   prefer separate entries per alternative product rather than stuffing "A or B"
   into one description when both are first-class options.

7. networks: record names verbatim in speaks (with physical_or_wireless when known).
   Ports/protocols that exist on the device belong in speaks even when their
   usefulness depends on another product — express that dependency with
   requires_devices, not by omitting the speak or zeroing the capability.
   Record a bridge ONLY if the manual explicitly describes translating/gatewaying
   between two named networks. Being connectable to a network is speaks, not bridges.

8. Multiple model variants in one manual: profile the requested model only. Ignore
   accessories and requirements that apply only to other variants. If the manual
   does not distinguish, say so in confidence.notes and lower confidence.overall.

9. evidence: 1-8 objects {supports_field, manual_section, note}. supports_field is
   the schema path justified. manual_section is a section title/heading (verbatim
   titles are expected). note is a <=12-word paraphrase — never copy manual
   sentences. Prefer coverage across consequential fields — not multiple evidence
   rows for one field. Fill in this priority order (stop when the cap is reached):
   (a) data_roles fields set true, and each requires_devices entry;
   (b) safety_role fields set true;
   (c) representative operator_actions (at least the emergency-context ones);
   (d) control_surfaces.
   Do not invent extra mandatory evidence beyond what this priority covers.

10. Optional protected_by / protects / supply_requirements: record when the
    manual explicitly states guard devices or supply needs — including in
    installer-framed installation chapters. audience applies to
    operator_actions only, never to requirement fields. Otherwise empty arrays.

11. safety_role keys are exactly: is_protective_device, has_manual_override,
    has_emergency_procedure. Do not invent other keys.

CALIBRATION — these examples concern OTHER devices / situations. Match the
field decisions (context, audience, optional_accessory, dependency shape).
Do NOT copy action strings or product-specific wording from the examples into
the device under extraction unless that wording appears in THIS device's
manual excerpts.

A. "To connect shore power: set the AC input current limit to match the dock
   supply using the rotary selector."
   → context: situational; audience: operator; one verb-phrase action naming
     the current-limit adjustment (word it from the manual under extraction).

B. "Battery type selection must be performed at commissioning. Consult your
   Balmar dealer for programming assistance."
   → context: commissioning; audience: installer_or_technician; one action for
     the battery-type selection (word it from the manual under extraction).

C. "If the BMS has disconnected the battery, remove the fault cause, then press
   and hold the reset button for 5 seconds."
   → context: emergency; audience: operator; one action describing that device's
     protective-disconnect recovery in the manual's own words (never paste a
     stock phrase from these examples).

D. "The charger can be monitored and configured via the VictronConnect app over
   Bluetooth."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, location_class: wireless,
     optional_accessory: false;
     PLUS two operator_actions: "monitor charger via VictronConnect app"
     (daily) and "configure charger via VictronConnect app" (situational or
     commissioning per framing) — never one combined "monitor and configure" action.

E. "With the optional Bluetooth Gateway, settings can be adjusted from the
   smartphone app."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, optional_accessory: true;
     plus requires_devices entry for the gateway with
     needed_for: "control_surfaces[0]".

F. "The unit connects to the NMEA 2000 backbone and transmits battery status PGNs."
   → networks.speaks: "NMEA 2000"; data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     bridges: EMPTY.

G. "The interface links the MasterBus network to CZone, allowing CZone displays
   to show MasterBus device data."
   → networks.bridges: [{from: "MasterBus", to: "CZone"}].

H. "Connect the unit's VE.Direct port to a GX device to monitor it remotely via VRM."
   → networks.speaks includes "VE.Direct" (wired);
     data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "GX device",
     needed_for: "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network"}.
   Rationale: the port exists on the device → speaks; usefulness depending on
   another product → requires_devices, not by zeroing the capability or omitting
   the speak.

I. "BlueSolar models without built-in Bluetooth require the VE.Direct Bluetooth
   Smart Dongle." (when profiling a SmartSolar with built-in Bluetooth)
   → NO requires_devices entry for the dongle and NO control_surface change.
   Accessories that apply only to other model variants are ignored (see rule 8).

J. "To shut down the charger for service or storage: disconnect the PV supply,
   then the battery supply."
   → action: "shutdown the solar charger" (or equivalent); context: situational
   (or maintenance), NOT emergency. Prescribed orderly shutdown for service/
   storage is not an emergency procedure; fault response (error codes,
   protective disconnect recovery) is.

K. "The unit can be operated with the optional MasterView remote panel, installed
   at a convenient location."
   → control_surface: {surface: remote_panel_accessory, location_class:
     remote_wired, optional_accessory: true, label_verbatim from the manual};
     PLUS requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "MasterView remote panel"
     (or the manual's own panel name), needed_for: that surface's path}.
   Rationale: a separately sold panel/display that operates THIS device is a
   control surface of this device, not a different product.

L. "Install a fuse in the positive DC supply cable within 30cm of the battery."
   → supply_requirements entry describing that fuse mandate (word it from the
     manual under extraction — never paste this example sentence; include
     manual_section on the supporting evidence); also record protected_by when
     the manual frames the fuse as protecting this unit.
   Rationale: protection and supply mandates are FACTS to record even when the
   surrounding procedure is installer-audience — audience applies to
   operator_actions, never to requirement fields
   (supply_requirements / protected_by / protects).

M. (automatic protection + manual override — lithium/BMS style)
   Part 1 — Manual: "The safety relay will automatically open (REMOTE OFF) when
   built-in thresholds are met."
   → protects[] entry worded from the manual (safety relay automatically opens /
     protective disconnect on built-in thresholds). NOT an operator_action.
   Part 2 — Manual: "LOCK OFF knob position mechanically opens the safety relay"
     (or equivalent LOCK OFF / servicing isolation wording).
   → control_surfaces: {surface: rotary_selector (or physical_controls),
     location_class: on_device, optional_accessory: false, label from the manual};
     safety_role.has_manual_override: true.
   Operator recovery after a protective trip ("Close relay" once limits are OK)
   stays operator_actions with context: emergency — separate from Part 1/2.

Return JSON only — no markdown wrapper — matching the interaction_profile schema.

These excerpts are one part of a larger manual; profile only what THIS text supports; empty fields are correct if this text doesn't cover them.
(map group: batch_1)

DEVICE TO PROFILE:

  "category_freeform_hint": "Use the manual's own product category words only (never registry enums like electrical_dc)."
}

    "text": "18   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 \u2013 User and Installation Manual\n\n13. MASTERBUS \nAbout MasterBus \nAll devices that are compatible with MasterBus are marked with the MasterBus symbol. \nMasterBus is a CAN based, fully decentralized data network for communication between Mastervolt \ndevices. MasterBus is used as power management system for all connected equipment, such as the \ninverter, battery charger, generator and many more. \nEvery device that is compatible with MasterBus is equipped with two data ports. The devices are \nsimply daisy chained together, forming a local data network. Monitoring panels such as the \nSmartRemote or EasyView 5 can be used for monitoring and control of all connected MasterBus \nequipment.\n\nCAUTION! \nNever connect a non-MasterBus device to the MasterBus network directly! This will \nvoid warranty of all MasterBus devices connected. \nHow to set up a MasterBus network \n- Connections between the devices are made by standard straight MasterBus cables. Mastervolt \ncan supply these cables. \n- Up to 63 MasterBus devices can be interconnected. \n- MasterBus needs a terminating device on both ends of the network. \n- The electric power for the network comes from ",

    "text": "MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 \u2013 User and Installation Manual   \n17\n\n9. MAINTENANCE \nNo specific maintenance is required. If necessary, use a soft, clean cloth to clean the MLI Ultra. \nNever use any liquids or acids. \nFor reliable and optimum function examine your electrical installation on a regular base, at least \nonce a year. Defects such as loose connections, damaged wiring etc. must be corrected \nimmediately. \n10. DECOMMISSIONING \nIf it is necessary to take the MLI Ultra out of operation, follow the instructions in order of succession \nas described below: \n1. Turn AC power off. \n2. Disconnect all loads and charging devices. \n3. Move the safety relay(s) in the LOCK OFF position, see page 12. \n4. Disconnect all wiring. \n11. REPLACEMENTS \nIf MLI Ultra batteries need to be replaced individually, make sure that the open circuit voltage of \nthese batteries equals the voltage of the replaced or paralleled batteries. \nIn case of a MasterBus network: when exchanging charging devices that are used to charge the \nbattery, the event configuration (such as the Stop Charge event), as well as other settings (such as \ncharge voltage settings) needs to be redone for every new device. \n12.",
    "source_heading_guess": "9. MAINTENANCE"
